era_believed.statement: Time Lords possess a binary vascular system with two fully functional hearts.
Regeneration allows a Time Lord to completely rebuild their cellular structure at the point of death.
The TARDIS travels through the Time Vortex to reach any point in space and time.
A broken chameleon circuit is the reason the TARDIS is permanently stuck as a 1960s police box.
The Daleks were genetically engineered by the scientist Davros on the planet Skaro.
Cybermen were originally humans who underwent forced cybernetic conversion to remove all emotion.
Weeping Angels are quantum-locked creatures that can only move when no one is observing them.
The sonic screwdriver can interface with almost any technology except deadlock seals and wood.
Gallifrey existed in the constellation of Kasterborous and was the homeworld of the Time Lords.
The Eye of Harmony is a collapsed star that provides the primary power source for all TARDISes.
Psychic paper displays whatever the holder wants the viewer to see, appearing as valid credentials.
The Time War was a devastating conflict fought between the Time Lords and the Daleks across all of history.
Rassilon founded Time Lord civilisation and invented time travel technology on Gallifrey.
The Master is a renegade Time Lord whose drumming madness was implanted by the Time Lords themselves.
UNIT is a military organisation established to defend Earth against extraterrestrial threats.
The Torchwood Institute was founded by Queen Victoria in 1879 to combat alien incursions into Britain.
A TARDIS is dimensionally transcendental, meaning its interior exists in a different dimension from its exterior.
Time Lord body temperature runs significantly lower than that of humans at around fifteen degrees Celsius.
The Untempered Schism is a gap in the fabric of reality through which all Time Lord children must gaze.
Artron energy is a form of temporal radiation that accumulates in the bodies of time travellers.
The Matrix on Gallifrey is a vast computer network containing the accumulated knowledge of all deceased Time Lords.
A standard Time Lord regeneration cycle grants exactly thirteen incarnations before the cycle is exhausted.
The TARDIS translation circuit automatically translates all alien languages for the pilot and passengers.
Dalek casings are made of bonded polycarbide armour and contain a mutated Kaled creature inside.
The Silence are a religious order whose members are instantly forgotten the moment you look away from them.
The Ood are a telepathic species who communicate through a shared psychic field called the Ood Brain.
Gallifreyan technology operates on block transfer computation, using pure mathematics to reshape reality.
The Sontarans are a clone warrior race whose entire civilisation is devoted to perpetual warfare.
Fixed points in time are events that cannot be altered without catastrophic damage to the timeline.
The Void is the empty space between parallel universes and anything that passes through it collects Void stuff.
Huon particles are an ancient form of energy that was present at the creation of the universe.
The Judoon are intergalactic police for hire who enforce laws across multiple planetary jurisdictions.
A perception filter causes observers to unconsciously ignore whatever object or person it conceals.
The Zygons are shapeshifting aliens who can perfectly replicate the appearance and memories of any human.
The Vashta Nerada are microscopic swarm predators that live in shadows and strip flesh from bone in seconds.
A Time Lord can enter a deep healing coma to recover from injuries that would kill any human.
The Silurians are an intelligent reptilian species that ruled Earth millions of years before humanity evolved.
The Pandorica was a legendary prison built by an alliance of alien races to contain the universe's greatest threat.
Temporal energy residue left by a TARDIS materialisation can be detected and tracked by advanced sensors.
The Moment is a sentient Time Lord weapon so powerful it developed a conscience and refused to be used.
Gallifrey was hidden in a pocket universe at the end of the Time War to save it from destruction.
The Cybermen's emotional inhibitor is the only thing preventing converted humans from going insane with horror.
Time Lords can recognise each other across regenerations through a unique telepathic signature.
Weeping Angels feed on temporal energy by sending victims back in time and consuming their unlived days.
A TARDIS is grown from a coral-like organic material on Gallifrey, not mechanically constructed.
The chameleon arch can rewrite a Time Lord's biology to make them fully human, hiding their identity completely.
Adipose are creatures made entirely from human fat, created by alien nursemaids using Earth's population.
The TARDIS contains a telepathic interface that allows its pilot to navigate by thought alone.
Bad Wolf was a message scattered through time and space by Rose Tyler after absorbing the Time Vortex.
The Ice Warriors are a Martian reptilian species who built an advanced civilisation beneath the Martian surface.
A paradox machine can be built from a cannibalised TARDIS to sustain causality violations indefinitely.
The Sisterhood of Karn guards the Sacred Flame that produces the Elixir of Life on the planet Karn.
Time Lords have a respiratory bypass system that allows them to survive without breathing for extended periods.
The Great Intelligence is a disembodied sentient entity that exists outside normal space-time.
Donna Noble became the DoctorDonna, a human-Time Lord metacrisis that gave her a Time Lord mind.
The Racnoss are an ancient species of giant arachnids that existed at the formation of the Earth.
A fob watch can store the complete biological identity of a Time Lord using the chameleon arch.
The Rift in Cardiff leaks temporal energy that the TARDIS uses to refuel when parked above it.
The Shadow Proclamation is an intergalactic governing body that enforces universal laws across multiple species.
Gallifreyan writing is circular and cannot be translated by the TARDIS for non-Time Lord passengers.
The Doctor's true name is a closely guarded secret that holds immense power at the Fields of Trenzalore.
The Slitheen are a criminal family from Raxacoricofallapatorius who disguise themselves inside human skin suits.
Void ships can exist outside time and space, carrying passengers between parallel universes undetected.
The TARDIS has an emergency programme that returns companions safely home if the Doctor is killed.
Omega was the stellar engineer who created the supernova that gave the Time Lords mastery over time travel.
The Carrionites are witch-like beings who manipulate reality through word-based science rather than mathematics.
A TARDIS key glows and heats up when the TARDIS is nearby or attempting to materialise.
The Toclafane were the last remnants of humanity from the far future, regressed to cybernetic killing spheres.
A Time Lord's regeneration produces massive energy that can be weaponised or redirected for other purposes.
The planet Mondas was Earth's twin world and the original homeworld of the Cybermen.
The Medusa Cascade is a rift in space-time that the Doctor sealed during one of his earliest adventures.
Regeneration energy stored in a severed hand can be used to grow a duplicate biological body.
The Cult of Skaro was an elite group of four Daleks authorised to imagine and develop new strategies.
The Confession Dial is a Time Lord device containing a pocket dimension used for final testimony before death.
Time Lord consciousness can be uploaded into the Matrix, preserving their mind after physical death.
The Autons are plastic mannequins animated by the Nestene Consciousness using its control over polymers.
A crack in time can erase people and events from history completely as if they never existed.
The TARDIS interior contains a swimming pool, a library, and an infinite number of reconfigurable rooms.
The Hand of Omega is a remote stellar manipulator created by Omega to control and detonate stars.
The Time Lords imposed a strict policy of non-interference in the affairs of other civilisations.
Clara Oswald was scattered across the Doctor's timeline as echoes to save him from the Great Intelligence.
The planet Trenzalore is the prophesied site of the Doctor's final battle and eventual grave.
The Hybrid is a Gallifreyan prophecy foretelling a creature of two warrior races that will destroy Gallifrey.
A Time Lord can share regeneration energy with others to heal otherwise fatal injuries.
The Time Lord Academy on Gallifrey trains young Gallifreyans over centuries before granting them the title.
The Dalek Emperor survived the Time War and rebuilt the Dalek race using harvested human genetic material.
The Cloister Wraiths guard the Matrix beneath Gallifrey's Capitol, trapping intruders for eternity.
River Song is the Doctor's wife and a human with Time Lord DNA who can regenerate.
The Genesis Ark is a Time Lord prison ship that is bigger on the inside and held millions of Daleks.
Gallifrey's second sun gives the sky a burnt orange hue visible from the slopes of Mount Perdition.
The TARDIS console room can be reconfigured and redecorated according to the pilot's preferences.
Chronon energy is the fundamental particle that underlies all temporal phenomena across the universe.
The Sontarans have a single vulnerability called the probic vent located on the back of their necks.
The Doctor stole a Type 40 TARDIS from the repair bays on Gallifrey when fleeing his home planet.
The Testimony Foundation captures people at the moment of death and preserves their memories as glass avatars.
The Stenza are a warrior race who take a tooth from each victim as a trophy embedded in their face.
The War Doctor was a secret incarnation who fought in the Time War before renouncing the name Doctor.
Davros was critically injured and placed in a life-support chair that sustained him for millennia.
The reality bomb created by Davros was designed to destroy all matter in every universe simultaneously.
Time Lord science allows the creation of dimensionally transcendental spaces bigger on the inside.
The Timeless Child was a being found beneath a boundary who granted regeneration to all of Gallifrey.
Dalekanium is a rare metallic compound used in Dalek casing construction that can withstand extreme forces.
The TARDIS has a hostile action displacement system that moves it away from danger automatically.
Time Lords can put themselves into a death-like trance that stops both hearts to fool observers.
The planet Skaro has a petrified jungle and a vast lake of mutations created by ancient nuclear wars.
The Black Guardian is a cosmic entity representing chaos and entropy who seeks to disrupt universal order.
Amy Pond's house had too many rooms because a crack in time had erased her family from existence.
The Face of Boe is a giant head in a jar who is one of the oldest beings in the universe.
Nitro-9 is an explosive compound invented by Ace that is powerful enough to damage Dalek casings.
The TARDIS has a zero room that provides a space completely isolated from the universe for healing.
The planet Midnight has an xtonic star whose radiation instantly kills any living thing on the surface.
Validium is a living metal created by Rassilon and Omega that serves as the ultimate defence for Gallifrey.
The TARDIS Cloister Bell rings as an alarm when the ship or the universe faces catastrophic danger.
The Blinovitch Limitation Effect prevents a person from touching their past self without discharging temporal energy.
Daleks draw power from static electricity channels built into their city floors on the planet Skaro.
The Impossible Planet orbits a black hole in a stable orbit maintained by a gravity funnel of unknown origin.
The neural relay in a Cyberman suit allows a dead human's consciousness to persist inside the metal shell.
The Master once used a laser screwdriver powered by the captured energy of a dying star.
The sonic screwdriver emits focused sound waves that can unlock doors, scan organisms, and hack computers.
Artron energy signatures left on companions allow the TARDIS to track them across time and space.
